design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed levels collecting story characters (R2-D2, Obi-Wan, Han Solo), manage character-specific resources, overcome platforming obstacles and enemies, progress toward Death Star finale
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (Han and Leia have single lives vs Luke's multiple lives) combined with level navigation difficulty and enemy encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Live the original Star Wars film experience as Luke Skywalker, assembling the rebel team and infiltrating the Death Star
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Asymmetric character mechanics create meaningful tradeoffs—Han and Leia are liabilities with one-hit deaths but presumably offer tactical advantages; game forces player engagement with film's complete narrative arc rather than optional storytelling

difficulty.lives_system: Luke has multiple lives; Han Solo and Leia have single lives each. Obi-Wan can resurrect Han/Leia up to five times (Game Gear/Master System require kill-resurrect cycles for 100% completion)
difficulty.checkpointing: Hub-world structure (Tatooine) allows revisiting levels; Death Star is linear progression point
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection creates difficulty variance—using Han/Leia increases risk significantly. Game Gear version adds exclusive desert levels with distinct difficulty profile

progression.upgrade_system: Character roster expansion (collect Luke→R2-D2→Obi-Wan→Han Solo→Leia); shields required for Falcon asteroid field section
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression—must collect specific characters in sequence before advancing to next major phase (Tatooine→Falcon→Death Star)
replayability_hooks: Multiple character playstyles with asymmetric mechanics
Game Gear version features exclusive levels and modified Tatooine structure
100% completion requires specific character resurrection sequences on Game Gear/Master System

world_building_techniques: Direct film adaptation with location-specific level design (Tatooine desert, Sandcrawler, cave, Mos Eisley bar, Falcon, Death Star)
Character collection mechanic reinforces film's party assembly narrative
Manual/instruction provided hint book for narrative clarity

notable_bosses: Death Star tractor beam generator (mechanic-based challenge)

faction_indicators: Imperial forces (Stormtroopers, Tusken Raiders as antagonists)
Rebel alliance (Luke, Obi-Wan, Han, Leia, R2-D2, C-3PO as collectible allies)
danger_signals: Single-life mechanic for Han/Leia creates tension around character survival
Tractor beam generator and Death Star infiltration represent existential rebel threat
environmental_storytelling: Tatooine desert hub establishes remote outpost setting
Mos Eisley bar establishes smuggler recruitment
Falcon cockpit switches to first-person for space sequence distinction
Death Star interior architecture reflects imperial scale and danger
